name: Billy Kyle
abstract: William Osborne Kyle (July 14, 1914 – February 23, 1966) was an American jazz pianist. He is perhaps best known as an accompanist.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Woody Herman and His Third Herd/ Louis Armstrong and His All Stars

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Holmes
about_quotes.evidence: There was Billy Kyle on piano... We had worked up at the 845 Club in the Bronx... the man hired Billy Kyle to come in with the band... It was practically the same men

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmie Crawford
about_quotes.evidence: Billy Kyle called me, and asked me did I want to do a show, a Broadway musical... So I went in that show

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Lucky had a good band. He had Charlie Shavers, Billy Kyle, he had Tab Smith in the band, Don Byas

about_quotes.speaker: Al Hall
about_quotes.evidence: When I was a violinist, Billy Kyle was my accompanist. And we used to play social events

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: When the band started it's first engagement in the Zanzibar club... Billy Kyle(piano)

about_quotes.speaker: Buck Clayton
about_quotes.evidence: And you know, sometimes you just can't get started. And they were in good shape. Maybe it was because they knew that they were running up against Basie.
